commit.message:
Add blog post: Why Multiple Models Matter - GitHub Copilot CLI's Subagent Architecture

This post explores how GitHub Copilot CLI enables parallel multi-model workflows through
subagents, allowing developers to mitigate blind spots by running multiple models (e.g.,
GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus) in parallel and consolidating their insights.

Key topics covered:
- Blind spot problem: every model has different weaknesses
- Spawning parallel reviews with /review command
- Workflow composition: /plan with live validation
- Rubber Duck experimental feature for automatic second opinions
- Cost efficiency: charged per user request, not per subagent
- Practical examples and getting started

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix kings living in foreign kingdoms — relocate to own capital

installNewKing() never set kingdomId or townId, so succession could
crown someone who lived in another kingdom and leave them there.
Now installNewKing() sets kingdomId to the ruled kingdom and moves
the king to their capital (or any owned town).

Save-load fixup extended: checks kingdomId matches ruled kingdom,
verifies townId is in own territory, relocates to capital if not.

Per-tick enforcement in tickKingMood: if king drifts to foreign town,
immediately relocates them back to their capital.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(simulate-pipeline): KV name from TF output + agent queue idempotency

Two validation/plan fixes:

1. KV name: Terraform computes the KV name differently from the script
   (strips hyphens, appends subscription suffix). After terraform apply,
   capture the actual name via 'terraform output -raw kv_name' and use it
   in the validation checks.

2. Agent queue idempotency: Before terraform plan, check if the project-scoped
   agent queue already exists in ADO via 'az devops invoke'. If it does, pass
   -var='ado_queue_exists=true' so Terraform uses a data source instead of
   trying to create it (which fails with 'Agent pool already exists').
   Same flag passed to terraform destroy.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add 14 missing action quest mechanics + king mood affects all decisions

ACTION_QUEST_MECHANICS: Added 14 entries for military (recruit_npcs,
stay_location, sabotage_enemy), economic (create_caravan, build_building),
diplomatic (diplomatic_mission), justice (capture_criminal, enforce_ban,
manhunt, arbitrate, patrol_roads, escort_npc, confiscate), and social
(mediate) quest types. Each has tickCost, goldCost, successBase, skillKey,
and narrative text.

King mood now affects quest generation: category weights (wrathful king
favors military/justice, jubilant favors economic/social), reward
generosity modifier, and urgency bias (stressed kings escalate urgency).

King mood integrated into all major engine decisions:
- War declarations: warMod multiplies war chance, affects conviction
- Peace-seeking: fearful kings sue faster, wrathful resist
- Alliances: fearful kings lower relation threshold for allies
- Conscription: conscriptMod multiplied into chance
- Bans: paranoid/wrathful ban more, jubilant/content unban more
- Military recruitment: conscriptMod scales peacetime recruiting
- Infrastructure: grieving/wrathful kings invest less
- Nationalization: wrathful/paranoid kings nationalize more
- Petitions: petitionMod multiplies player petition success chance

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Add Claude Desktop Cowork session support (#572)

* feat: add Claude Desktop Cowork session support

Add support for Claude Desktop Cowork (local agent mode) sessions. These are stored as nested JSONL files under %LOCALAPPDATA%\Packages\Claude_pzs8sxrjxfjjc\LocalCache\Roaming\Claude\local-agent-mode-sessions\ on Windows with actual API token counts (same format as Claude Code).

Changes:
- New ClaudeDesktopCoworkDataAccess class (vscode-extension/src/claudedesktop.ts)
- Restrict isClaudeCodeSessionFile() to paths strictly under ~/.claude/projects/ to avoid false-positives on cowork session paths
- Wire cowork into sessionDiscovery, extension, usageAnalysis (vscode-extension)
- Wire cowork into helpers and stats command (cli)
- Update README and docs/cli/README.md with new supported source

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: label Cowork paths as 'Claude Desktop Cowork' in workspaceHelpers

Both getEditorTypeFromPath and detectEditorSource used a broad
'/.claude/projects/' substring check. Cowork session JSONL paths
contain that sub-path, so they were mislabeled as 'Claude Code'.

Add '/local-agent-mode-sessions/' guard before '/.claude/projects/'
in both functions to return the correct display label.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add claudeDesktopCowork to getModelUsageFromSession Pick type

The Pick<UsageAnalysisDeps, ...> on getModelUsageFromSession was missing
'claudeDesktopCowork', causing TS2339 errors in CI type checking.

Also adds workspaceHelpers.ts fix from previous commit to ensure
correct editor labels.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct Cowork session count, editor labels, and display

Three issues found via live testing:

1. audit.jsonl excluded: Each Cowork session dir has both an audit.jsonl
   (HMAC-signed audit trail) and a UUID-named .jsonl (actual Claude API
   session data). We were picking up both, doubling the session count from
   3 to 8. Fixed by skipping files named 'audit.jsonl' in walkForJsonlFiles.

2. agent/ directory excluded: The 'agent/' subdirectory holds background
   Cowork agent sessions (local_ditto_*) that the user never creates
   directly and that have no title. Fixed by skipping 'agent' directories
   during the walk.

3. enrichDetailsWithEditorInfo missing Cowork/ClaudeCode guards: Without
   an explicit guard, Cowork (and Claude Code) sessions fell through to the
   generic 'User' path-splitting logic, resolved editorRoot to 'C:' on
   Windows, and got labelled 'Unknown'. Added guards for both editors so
   editorName is set correctly before the generic fallback runs.

4. getEditorIcon: Added 'Claude Code' and 'Claude Desktop Cowork' entries
   so the orange circle icon shows instead of the generic 📝 fallback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Cowork token/title/interactions/log-viewer display bugs

- Fix getCoworkModelUsage: was checking event.type !== 'assistant' but
  assistant messages have no top-level type; now checks event.message?.role
- Fix getSessionFileDetails: add Cowork branch before generic file read so
  title, interactions, and timestamps come from metadata + JSONL instead of
  falling through to Copilot-CLI handler
- Fix getSessionLogData: add Cowork branch that builds ChatTurns from
  readCoworkEvents(), including actual token usage and tool calls from
  assistant message content blocks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Cowork streaming fragment dedup and turn-splitting bugs

- Fix token counting: streaming fragments have stop_reason='' (empty string)
  not null/undefined. Change filter to !stop_reason (falsy) to catch all
  three, so only final events (stop_reason='tool_use'/'end_turn') are counted.
  Also align type check: ALL Cowork assistant events have type:'assistant'
  at top level (both fragments and finals).

- Fix log viewer turn count: tool-result callbacks are stored as type:'user'
  but content is [{type:'tool_result',...}] not text. Add isRealUserMessage()
  guard matching countCoworkInteractions logic — only start a new turn when
  content is text with no tool_result blocks. This reduces 30 fake turns to
  the correct 3 actual human interactions.

- Only collect final assistant events (stop_reason truthy) in pendingAssistant
  array — streaming fragments have no meaningful content or token data.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: document Cowork JSONL format in claudedesktop.ts header

Captures learnings from live filesystem investigation:
- stop_reason is '' (empty string) on streaming fragments, not null/undefined
- All assistant events (streaming + final) have type:'assistant' at top level
- Tool-result user events vs real human turns (parentUuid + content checks)
- requestId dedup strategy for token counting

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
sessions: show restore button instead of checkmark for done sessions (#309058)

* sessions: show restore button instead of checkmark for done sessions

When a session is already marked as done, the checkmark button in the
command center was still shown but did nothing on click. Now:

- Hide the 'Mark as Done' checkmark when the active session is archived
- Show the existing 'Restore' button (discard icon) in its place
- Track active session archived state via IsActiveSessionArchivedContext

Fixes #307712

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: track previous archived state for false→true transitions

Use a mutable wasArchived variable instead of a constant so that
openNewSessionView() fires correctly on restore→re-archive flows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
r600: B-Track measurement results — SQ counters, BO profiling, probe status

Hardware SQ counter profiling confirms CPU Starvation Theorem:
- ALU/TEX ratio 22:1 to 54:1 (GPU overwhelmingly ALU-bound)
- TA_BUSY=0 at register level (texture never a bottleneck)
- texture 50%, vertex 40%, cube 100% CPU-starved

BO count profiling: 5-11 pre-existing BOs per draw (not per-frame allocs).
b5-ringbuf CLOSED — marginal value, GEM_WAIT_IDLE is real bottleneck.

OpenCL latency probes BLOCKED by Rusticl RAT write crash (Phase 4A).
GL probes confirmed working via PIGLIT_PLATFORM=surfaceless_egl.

4 rubber-duck reviews converge: state-refactor low value, ringbuf marginal.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix CLR->COM RCW ownership tracking on the slow path (#126731)

Fixes #126619.

This was a regression introduced in
https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/pull/114609

When `System.StubHelpers.GetCOMIPFromRCW()` misses the fast path on a
native-created thread, the slow path can create OLE TLS and still
resolve the COM interface pointer from the RCW cache. Those cache hits
are borrowed and should not be released by the stub cleanup path.

This change moves the `pfNeedsRelease` decision to the slow helper so it
can distinguish between:
- RCW cache hits after OLE TLS initialization
- freshly acquired interface pointers from
`ComObject::GetComIPFromRCWThrowing`

Testing:
- verified against the local repro from #126619

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: centralize color palette in shared/colors.js

Create single source of truth for all colors:
- shared/colors.js: hex values with semantic names (brand, surface, status, etc.)
- shared/palette.ts: RGBA conversion helpers for canvas rendering
- tailwind.config.js: imports shared colors for CSS classes
- shared/config.ts: re-exports RGBA colors from palette

Replace all hardcoded Tailwind color classes (red-400, gray-500, etc.)
with semantic names (bg-brand, text-status-ready, text-neutral-500, etc.)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace instant 'Report Complete' with real action quest mechanics

One-off action quests (espionage, social, corrupt) now require actual
player investment instead of clicking a free completion button:

- Gold cost: each action requires gold for supplies/bribes/preparations
- Time cost: actions advance game time (3-15 days depending on type)
- Success roll: based on base chance + relevant skill branch bonuses
- Failure: gold and time are lost, player must retry
- Location requirements: some actions (tutor_heir, rig_election, etc.)
  require being in the kingdom capital

Added ACTION_QUEST_MECHANICS config with 16 action types:
  Espionage: decode, intercept, investigate, disinformation, sabotage
  Social: arrange_marriage, tutor_heir, sway_vote, advise_succession
  Corrupt: eliminate_rival, silence_witness, forge_evidence, seize_assets,
           intimidate, frame_merchant, rig_election

Each has unique narrative text, action labels, costs, and success rates.
UI shows: gold cost, time cost, location requirement, base success chance,
attempt count, and a detailed result modal on success/failure.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(simulate-pipeline): include KV_SUFFIX in SA name to avoid cross-run conflicts

The SA name was truncating PROJECT_NAME to 10 chars, which cut off the
random KV_SUFFIX. This made the SA name deterministic across runs, causing
'StorageAccountAlreadyExists' errors when a previous --no-destroy run left
the SA behind.

Fix: build SA name from full project name then truncate the whole string
to 24 chars (Azure SA name limit), preserving the KV_SUFFIX.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  state, X11 wiring, GTT allocation, specialized sync, wait-only IB,
  submission gate). TERAKAN_X11_EXPERIMENTAL_HW_WAIT gating confirmed.
- WSI Acquire Semaphore RCA: 4 boundary classes correctly decomposed,
  pre-existing semaphore-lifetime issue confirmed separate from HW wait.
- Robustness metadata: full end-to-end pipeline confirmed (NIR write
  guard emission, KCACHE bank 14 metadata population with trash page,
  per-stage binding at draw/dispatch time, SSBO read robustness at zero
  ALU cost via HW TEX/VTX OOB-returns-zero).
- Scratch assembly: CF_OP_MEM_SCRATCH (write, cheap) and LoadFromScratch

RX_BUF (512) is the read chunk size, not the hardware FIFO size.
Ref: MAX-M10S Data Sheet UBX-20035208.

commit.message:
r600: CPU Starvation Theorem formalization and per-benchmark RCA

Formal findings from triple RCA investigation on AMD E-300 APU:

CPU Starvation Theorem:
- IPC = 0.21 (79% stalled), 27% L2 cache miss, 21% branch mispredict
- GPU utilization 70.83% composite; sh% == gpu% everywhere (GPU is
  shader-busy when fed, all idle time is CPU starvation)
- ta% = 0% on all benchmarks including texture — TEX fetch is never
  the bottleneck
- Five starvation mechanisms identified (DRM ioctl, GEM_WAIT_IDLE,
  Bobcat pipeline stalls, LTO code bloat, thermal coupling)
- Six testable hypotheses with expected outcomes

Per-Benchmark RCA:
- shading:phong 635 FPS (GPU 66%) — signal wait 1.3ms (82% of frame)
- texture 808 FPS (GPU 51%) — CPU-starved, GPU idle 49%
- vertex 771 FPS (GPU 68%) — highest VGT util, 32% starvation gap
- Theoretical ceilings: 962 / 1584 / 1134 FPS at 100% GPU utilization

Submit time regression (70-95µs vs prior 10-18µs target):
- Root cause: added BO references, conservative flush packets,
  alloca/memcpy overhead from fence elision infrastructure
- Recovery: shader suballoc, descriptor consolidation, persistent ring

Data archived at:
  x130e:/home/eirikr/workspaces/mesa/maps/telemetry/per_benchmark_gpu_rca_20260410/

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fall back to first user message as title for untitled Copilot CLI sessions

When a Copilot CLI session has no rename_session tool call the title was
left blank (shown as '(Empty session)'). Now, if no explicit title is
found after scanning all events.jsonl lines, the first user.message
content is used as a fallback (truncated to 60 chars + ellipsis).

Applied in both extractSessionMetadata and getSessionFileDetails.
Bump CACHE_VERSION to 36 to invalidate stale cached entries that lack
the fallback title.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(capture): pick AVFoundation device by display index, handle multi-monitor (#65)

- Replace single-match detectScreenCaptureDeviceIndex with getScreenCaptureDeviceIndex(ffmpegPath, displayIndex)
  that collects ALL 'Capture screen' devices and selects matched[displayIndex]
- Add getWindowDisplayIndex() to platform.ts — uses NSScreen via osascript to find
  which physical display VS Code's window is currently on (0-based index)
- Cache device lookup per displayIndex (Map<number,string>) instead of a single module var
- Falls back to matched[0] if displayIndex out of range, then to '1' if no devices found
- darwin args keep AVFOUNDATION_NATIVE_FRAMERATE=60 and fps=${fps}, vf filter (from PR #64)
- Export getWindowDisplayIndex from src/platform/index.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(simulate-pipeline): swap tfvars for TFLint step to avoid template syntax errors

TFLint auto-loads terraform.tfvars. In the skeleton/ directory this file
contains Backstage template syntax (${{values.*}}) which is not valid HCL,
causing TFLint to fail with 'Invalid character' errors.

Fix: temporarily replace terraform.tfvars with tests/test.tfvars (which has
real values) before running TFLint, then restore the original. This mirrors
what happens in the actual pipeline where the file is already rendered.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
